findings.description: u_0003 ('Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning') is not represented by any candidate rule in this group — an entire accessibility directive is missing from the cluster.

payload.statement: Do not rely on color alone to convey meaning; pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

findings.description: This rule merges two semantically distinct directives: (1) an affirmative list of known-safe color/background pairings (positive guidance, should/must polarity), and (2) a prohibition on gold/sunshine text on white (must_not polarity). Assigning a single must_not polarity to the whole rule misrepresents the positive safe-pairs guidance.

new_rules.statement: Prefer known-safe text/background pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy.
Do not use Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast); golds are reserved for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

findings.description: The rule states a hard testing requirement (WCAG AA thresholds) but omits the governance facet specifying verification method/tool (webaim.com or equivalent) named in the source, leaving compliance unauditable.

governance.threshold: WCAG AA: 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text (≥24px or 19px bold)

findings.description: Class is set to color_application only, but the gold/sunshine-on-white prohibition is also an accessibility contrast concern (fails contrast per heading context), suggesting a missed relation to the sibling contrast-testing rule.
